title: Gonocormus novoguineensis
taxonomy: Gonocormus novoguineensis is classified as a species within the family Apocynaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants. This classification places Gonocormus novoguineensis among other plants that are commonly known as dogbanes or oleanders. The genus Gonocormus is part of the subfamily Rauvolfioideae, which contains plants that are primarily native to tropical regions of the world. Further classification of Gonocormus novoguineensis within the plant kingdom places it as a member of the kingdom Plantae, the phylum Angiospermophyta, and the class Magnoliopsida. The specific classification of Gonocormus novoguineensis is based on its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other plant species.

intro: Gonocormus novoguineensis, also known as the New Guinea gonocormus, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of New Guinea. The New Guinea gonocormus has been found to possess various health benefits, including its potential to inhibit the growth of certain microorganisms and its anti-inflammatory properties, which may aid in the treatment of wounds and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Gonocormus novoguineensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditional herbal preparations made with Gonocormus novoguineensis include infusions and tinctures, often used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and infections in the local communities of New Guinea.
